hub_subtitle: Conversação para viagens, listening de verdade e reuniões internacionais — rumo a Nova York, setembro de 2026

lesson.menu_title: What Would You Do? -- Dream Trips, Hypotheticals, and the Week You Would Choose
lesson.menu_desc: Do problema real ao sonho real: second conditional, I wish e a semana perfeita em Nova York -- 37 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- What Would You Do?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Lucimara | Aula 5 -- What Would You Do?

listenings.text: Hi Lucimara, this is Nathan from Hudson Travel, returning your call about September. I have put together two versions of that week, and honestly, they are very different. Version one is the safe one. You land at Kennedy on Sunday, you stay in Midtown, and everything is ten minutes from the hotel. If I were you, I would not take that one. Version two costs almost the same, but it puts you in Brooklyn for two nights. If you stayed in Brooklyn, you would see the part of the city that most of my clients never see, and you would still be twenty minutes from your meeting. Now, about the flight. If you took the red-eye on Saturday, you would land at seven in the morning and you would gain a whole day. If you would rather sleep in a real bed, take the Sunday flight, and we lose Sunday. One last thing. If I had your budget, I would not splurge on the room. I would cut back on the hotel and put the money into the food. Call me back and tell me which version sounds like you.

Welcome back to The Slow Traveler. Today I want to ask you the question I ask every guest, and this week I am going to answer it myself. If you had seven free days, and money was not the problem, where would you go, and what would you actually do? Here is my honest answer. I would not go anywhere new. I would go back to New York, and I would do it completely differently. I wish I were braver when I travel. I wish I had more days and fewer plans. So this time, if I had that week, I would give myself one rule and only one: one neighborhood a day, on foot, off the beaten path. I would take the ferry instead of the train, because the ferry is a detour, and detours are where the trip actually happens. I would eat where the line is longest. And if a stranger invited me somewhere, I would say yes. My producer says that is not a plan, that is a mood. She would rather have a spreadsheet. But I think the best week of your life is never the one you organized. It is the one you were brave enough to leave open.

items.situation: The travel agent asks you the big question: if you had one free week in New York, what would you do with it?
tips: If I had one free week, I would walk a different neighborhood every day.
Answer with a full second conditional -- If + past simple, would + verb.

items.situation: Two flights, same price. One leaves at midnight and lands at seven. Say what you prefer, and why.
tips: I would rather take the red-eye -- if I landed at seven, I would gain a whole day.
Would rather + base verb: I would rather arrive early than lose a Sunday.

items.situation: Your budget is generous, but not unlimited. Say where you would spend more, and where you would spend less.
tips: I would splurge on two great dinners, and I would cut back on the hotel.
If we cut back on taxis, we would stretch the budget into one more night.

items.situation: A colleague at the Lyon fair offers you a spontaneous side trip on Saturday. React, and say what you would do with the day.
tips: If I had Saturday free, I would take the detour with you -- I love an unplanned day.
In an ideal world, I would go somewhere off the beaten path, not to another museum.

items.situation: Someone asks you what you would change about your English. Answer with a wish.
tips: I wish I understood every word a New Yorker said.
I wish I had more time to listen -- but I do have eighty minutes in the car every day.

items.situation: Now the real one: your flight tonight is canceled. Careful -- this one can actually happen.
tips: If my flight is canceled, I will rebook at the desk and ask for a voucher.
Real and possible = first conditional (will). Imagined = second conditional (would).
